module_title: Generative AI and Large Language Models-L
companion_quiz: Generative AI and Large Language Models-Q

sources.citation: Vaswani et al. Attention Is All You Need. NeurIPS, 2017.

learning_objectives: L1: Define generative AI and distinguish it from discriminative AI.
L1: Identify the three LLM training stages: pre-training, SFT, and RLHF.
L1: Name four generative model families: LLMs, GANs, VAEs, diffusion models.
L1: Define prompt, context window, temperature, and token in LLM context.
L2: Explain how autoregressive next-token prediction produces coherent text.
L2: Describe how RLHF aligns LLM outputs with human preferences.
L2: Explain the role of temperature and top-p sampling in controlling diversity.
L2: Compare zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuning for adapting LLMs to new tasks.
L3: Write an effective prompt using chain-of-thought and role-prompting techniques.
L3: Evaluate an LLM output for hallucination, bias, and misuse and propose mitigations.

concepts.title: Generative vs Discriminative
concepts.explanation: Discriminative: learn P(y|x) — classify existing data. Generative: learn P(x) or P(x|y) — generate new data.
concepts.worked_example: Discriminative: BERT → 'I loved it' → POSITIVE. Generative: GPT → 'I loved it because' → '...the characters felt so real'.

concepts.title: Four Generative Families
concepts.explanation: LLMs: Transformer decoder, autoregressive. GANs: generator vs discriminator — sharp images, unstable training. VAEs: smooth latent space, blurry. Diffusion: reverse noise — SOTA image quality.
concepts.worked_example: StyleGAN: photorealistic faces. Stable Diffusion: diverse high-quality images. DALL-E 3: complex prompt interpretation.

concepts.title: Autoregressive Generation
concepts.explanation: Trained on next-token prediction. Context window: 4K–200K tokens. Temperature: T→0 deterministic, T→∞ random. Top-p: sample from smallest set with cumulative prob ≥p.
concepts.worked_example: 'The capital of France is': Paris=0.91. T=0.1: Paris ~99.9%. T=2.0: more diverse, sometimes wrong.

concepts.title: Scale and Emergent Abilities
concepts.explanation: Scaling laws: performance improves predictably with parameters, data, compute. Emergent abilities appear abruptly at scale — few-shot learning, chain-of-thought, code generation.
concepts.worked_example: GPT-2 (1.5B) fails 3-digit addition. GPT-3 (175B) solves reliably with few-shot examples.

concepts.title: Three Training Stages
concepts.explanation: Stage 1 Pre-training: next-token prediction on trillions of tokens — base model. Stage 2 SFT: train on (prompt, ideal response) pairs. Stage 3 RLHF: rank responses → reward model → PPO optimisation.
concepts.worked_example: RLHF reduced harmful outputs 25× vs base model (Ouyang et al., 2022).

concepts.title: Zero-shot, Few-shot, Fine-tuning
concepts.explanation: Zero-shot: prompt only. Few-shot: 2–10 examples in context. Fine-tuning: update weights — highest performance. LoRA: train small adapters, 100× fewer params. CoT: 'think step by step' improves reasoning.
concepts.worked_example: Medical coding: zero-shot 61%, few-shot 74%, fine-tuned 91%. CoT zero-shot 69%.

concepts.title: Hallucination, Bias, and Governance
concepts.explanation: Hallucination: fluent but factually wrong. Fix: RAG, self-consistency, citations. Bias: reflects training data. Mitigations: model cards, usage policies, watermarking, output filtering.
concepts.worked_example: RAG: retrieve central bank document → answer from retrieved text. Without RAG: cites stale training data.
